news.title: Consumer sentiment hits fresh record low in May as Iran war fuels inflation worries
news.summary: Consumer sentiment has tumbled to a fresh record low in May. Fears of higher prices grow due to the U.S.-Iran war and elevated oil prices.

news.title: Trump defends DOJ fund after Senate Republicans push back
news.summary: President Trump dropped his $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS in exchange for the DOJ creating a $1.8 billion fund to compensate alleged "lawfare" victims.

news.title: Watch: Trump will swear in Kevin Warsh as Fed chair as he seeks interest rate cuts
news.summary: Despite President Trump's demands for lower interest rates, markets are betting the Fed will stay on hold through most, if not all, of 2026.

news.title: Despite murky legal landscape, companies are undeterred in their prediction market investments
news.summary: Companies reiterated plans to growing their prediction markets businesses in recent earnings calls even as a regulatory debate continues. Companies reiterated plans for growth in prediction markets in recent Earnings calls.

news.title: Trump said he'd 'remember' companies that didn't apply for tariff refunds. Many of them are anyway
news.summary: Walmart confirmed to CNBC it has sought a tariff refund. It plans to use any money it receives back to invest in lower prices for shoppers.

news.title: I inherited a house. My CPA says I should sell within a year to avoid capital gains. Is he right?
news.summary: “We plan to sell it to another family member for the appraised value,” the family says. “We’re not selling it to anyone else. We just want to keep it.”

news.title: Annuity payouts are the highest they’ve been in years. Thanks, inflation.
news.summary: A silver lining to the cloud of high interest rates. The U.S. Federal Reserve is raising interest rates for the first time in more than a decade.

news.title: I’m in my 30s and half my money is in Vanguard’s Information Technology ETF. Is that risky?
news.summary: “I was told as a kid to just put money into index funds or individual stocks, leave them alone and never sell,” he says.

news.title: Nvidia can deliver chips — but it can’t buy Big Tech out of its credit and power-grid crisis
news.summary: Corporate profits can’t fix a chaotic trade war with China, climbing credit premiums and AI infrastructure limits, says the IMF.

news.title: An adviser told me I can save 35% of the taxes on a Roth conversion. Is that true?
news.summary: If it sounds too good to be true, it probably is. The age-old rule applies: If it looks too good, it most likely is.
